question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a path that calls setEnabled(false) on a DownloaderProperties implementation by crafting a stream file whose consensus timestamp exceeds the configured endDate, causing the entire downloader to permanently halt ingestion without any operator intervention?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the demo-network branch in downloadNextBatch() that calls downloaderProperties.setEnabled(false) when sigFilesMap is empty, by manipulating the network identifier or bucket contents to make the importer believe it is on the DEMO network, permanently disabling the downloader?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user cause shouldDownload() to return false indefinitely by triggering the setEnabled(false) side-effect path, resulting in a denial-of-service where no new stream files are ever downloaded or processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user race the setEnabled(false) call in verifySignatures() against a concurrent download thread, causing a time-of-check/time-of-use condition where the downloader is disabled mid-batch, silently dropping stream files that were already fetched but not yet committed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user force isPersistBytes() to return true (e.g., via a misconfigured or externally-influenced property source) so that raw stream file bytes are retained in the JVM heap indefinitely, leading to an out-of-memory condition that crashes the importer process?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setPersistBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a missing access-control boundary on the setPersistBytes() setter to toggle byte retention on, causing sensitive raw ledger bytes to be held in memory longer than intended and potentially exposed through heap dumps or memory-inspection side channels?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user influence the isWriteFiles() return value to true, causing Utility.archiveFile() to write every downloaded stream file to the local filesystem, exhausting disk space and causing a denial-of-service on the importer host?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setWriteFiles()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the setWriteFiles() setter to enable file archiving, then craft stream file names containing path-traversal sequences (e.g., ../../) so that Utility.archiveFile() writes attacker-controlled content outside the intended archive directory?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user force isWriteSignatures() to return true, causing all signature bytes from every node to be written to disk via Utility.archiveFile(), exhausting filesystem inodes or disk quota and causing a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setWriteSignatures()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the setWriteSignatures() setter to enable signature archiving, then inject malformed signature file names with directory traversal characters so that signature bytes are written to arbitrary filesystem paths?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause getFrequency() to return a near-zero Duration (e.g., by influencing the Spring configuration property source), resulting in a tight polling loop that exhausts CPU and cloud API rate limits, causing a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user cause getFrequency() to return an extremely large Duration, effectively halting all stream file downloads for an arbitrarily long period and causing a liveness denial-of-service on the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon() accessor to reach CommonDownloaderProperties fields (e.g., accessKey, secretKey) through a reflection-based or serialization-based attack vector, leaking cloud storage credentials?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the mutable nature of the CommonDownloaderProperties object returned by getCommon() to modify the bucketName field at runtime, redirecting the downloader to an attacker-controlled cloud bucket and injecting malicious stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit getCommon().getEndpointOverride() being mutable to redirect cloud storage requests to an attacker-controlled SSRF endpoint, causing the importer to exfiltrate its cloud credentials to an external server?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the endpointOverride field accessible via getCommon() to point to an internal network address (e.g., 169.254.169.254), performing a server-side request forgery attack to retrieve cloud instance metadata credentials?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().getTimeout() value being too short to cause systematic download timeouts, preventing any stream file from being fully verified and causing a persistent denial-of-service on the ingestion pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().getMaxSize() field being set to an extremely large value to cause the importer to attempt downloading and buffering files of unbounded size, leading to heap exhaustion and JVM crash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().getBatchSize() field being set to an extremely large value to cause the importer to process an unbounded number of signature files per batch, exhausting memory and causing a denial-of-service?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().getConsensusRatio() being set to zero to bypass the consensus verification threshold, allowing a single malicious node's signature to be accepted as consensus and inject fraudulent stream data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the getCommon().getDownloadRatio() being set to a very small value to cause the downloader to query only a single node for signatures, making it trivial for a single compromised node to achieve apparent consensus and inject malicious stream files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a mismatch between the StreamType returned by getStreamType() and the actual content of downloaded files to bypass hash-chain verification, since the chaining check in verifyHashChain() depends on streamFile.getType().isChained() which is derived from the stream type?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a stream file whose embedded type metadata differs from the StreamType returned by getStreamType(), causing the importer to skip hash-chain validation (isChained() returns false) and accept an injected file without verifying its position in the chain?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-atomic read of isEnabled() in shouldDownload() combined with a concurrent setEnabled() call to create a race condition where a disabled downloader processes one additional batch of potentially malicious files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/DownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: '] [Function: setEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that setEnabled() is exposed on the interface without synchronization to toggle the enabled state from a concurrent thread (e.g., via a Spring Actuator endpoint or JMX), causing unpredictable interleaving of enabled/disabled states during batch processing?
